title: Fix streaming progressiveness (buffering)
description: The local facade's streaming implementation buffers upstream tokens and emits them all at once at the end instead of streaming them progressively as they arrive. This fails test_parity_chat_stream_is_progressive.

title: Fix $.choices[0].message.tool_calls parity drift
description: In non-streaming chat completions, when tool_calls is absent, local facade returns 'tool_calls': null instead of omitting it completely, which causes parity drift with real OpenRouter.

title: Sign off: pre-publish review complete
description: Final sign-off task under the pre-publish review epic (fan-out-openrouter-1pg). Mark this closed only once all review findings have been resolved and the codebase is clean to publish.

title: Create public GitHub repo and push
description: Once the pre-publish review epic (fan-out-openrouter-1pg) is complete and all findings are resolved, create the public GitHub repository and push the main branch. Steps: create repo with gh repo create, set description and topics, push, verify CI passes on GitHub.

title: Pre-publish review: hunt for anything embarrassing or accidental
description: Do a thorough sweep of the entire codebase before making the repo public. Check for: hardcoded secrets or API keys, debug print statements left in, TODO/FIXME/HACK comments that shouldn't be public, test fixtures or data with real credentials, .env or secret files accidentally tracked, anything in git history that shouldn't be there, embarrassing WIP code, and anything that contradicts the public-facing README.

title: Write policy configuration guide
description: Create docs/policies.md explaining how to configure fanout_policies.json, candidate_models, fanout_count, and virtual_model.

title: Add Docker deployment support and docs
description: Create Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, and docs/deployment.md for running the proxy in production.

title: Write architecture documentation
description: Create docs/architecture.md explaining how the proxy works, the fan-out synthesis process, and the orchestrator.

title: Add beads (bd) workflow commands to justfile
description: Add commands to the justfile for common beads interactions (e.g.  to do git pull --rebase, bd dolt push, git push). Verify the workflow handles edge cases cleanly.

title: Create base justfile with core development commands
description: Add a justfile with the core commands needed for daily development: format, lint, test, and start the local server. Manually test each command to ensure they work out of the box.

title: Epic: Add a comprehensive and easy to use justfile
description: Create a comprehensive justfile based on the most common developer workflows for this project. This should include commands for testing, linting, formatting, running the server, and bd (beads) interactions. Ensure all commands are proven out with manual executions and actively hunt for bugs/edge cases.

title: Document Opencode configuration in README
description: Add a section to the project README.md documenting how to configure the opencode CLI to use the local facade. The documentation should include the JSON provider configuration and an example command of how to invoke it, as recorded in the parent epic's notes.

title: Implement opencode read-only code operations smoke test
description: Extend the opencode smoke harness (`tests/test_opencode_smoke.py`) to include a test that verifies `opencode run` can successfully perform read-only tool calls (like reading files, grepping, or globbing) against a mock codebase using the `fanout/minimal` model through the local facade. Requires writing a file into the test's isolated workspace, prompting opencode to read it, and asserting the model returns the file's contents.
notes: Started investigating opencode test hangs. Test opencode_run_round_trip hangs with 180s timeout. Noticed two POST requests to chat/completions in uvicorn log. Suspect opencode is either looping or ai-sdk stream termination parity issue.

title: [epic] Opencode Integration & Smoke Testing
description: Track the integration, testing, and documentation of the fan-out-openrouter facade as an OpenAI-compatible provider for the Opencode CLI.

title: Pre-publish: redact rotated API key from git history via git-filter-repo
Pre-publish: fill in empty placeholder sections in CLAUDE.md
Fix: move stray import random to top of orchestrator.py (ruff E402)
Add parity tests: ad-hoc fan-out via inline models array in request body
Add parity tests: pass-through routing (real model ID bypasses fan-out)
Per-request model routing: real model ID -> pass-through; models array -> ad-hoc fan-out
Replace google/gemini-3-flash-preview with anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5 in tests and policy — haiku supports prompt caching, gemini provider does not
Skip live tests in CI
description: In the public GitHub repo, we shouldn't run real OpenRouter API calls to save costs and avoid secret exposure. Added conftest.py to automatically skip live tests when CI=true.

title: Ensure stream keep-alive comments match OpenRouter
description: OpenRouter emits `: OPENROUTER PROCESSING\n\n` comments at the start of a stream or during processing. Our facade currently doesn't forward or synthesize these keep-alives, leading to parity gaps.

title: Epic: Comprehensive Documentation Site
description: Create a docs/ folder and establish comprehensive documentation. This should include architecture explanations, policy configuration guides (explaining fanout_policies.json), and deployment instructions (e.g., Dockerfile, Docker Compose).

title: Epic: Pre-Release Critical Codebase Review
description: Review the entire repository from the perspective of a skeptical, critical engineer browsing GitHub. Identify and clean up any embarrassing, accidental, stupid, or vacuous code, comments, dead files, or configurations that legitimately shouldn't be in a public repository.
close_reason: Removed test_stream.py which contained a leaked API key and was an ad-hoc top-level script.

title: Create GitHub Action for CI
description: Add a basic GitHub Actions workflow file to run the test suite and linting automatically on pull requests and pushes to main.

title: Add License File
description: Add an appropriate open-source license file (e.g., MIT, Apache 2.0) to the repository.

title: Implement Logging and Telemetry
description: Ensure sufficient logging is in place to help debug issues in production. This shouldn't leak sensitive information like API keys.

title: Add Missing Tests for Edge Cases
description: Review the current test suite and identify any critical edge cases or failure modes not yet covered. This includes error handling from OpenRouter, malformed requests, and extreme fan-out scenarios.

title: Code Quality and Linting Pass
description: Run Ruff (or other configured linters) across the entire codebase to ensure consistent code style and identify any potential issues or unused imports.

title: Review and Refine README.md
description: Ensure the README is comprehensive, accurate, and professional. It should cover installation, usage, configuration, and a clear description of the project's purpose and architecture. Also, add instructions for contributing and setting up the development environment.

title: Epic: Prepare Project for GitHub Publication
description: This epic tracks the incremental steps necessary to polish, document, and stabilize the project for public release on GitHub. Note: This epic will NOT actually push the code to GitHub, but only prepare the codebase to that standard.

title: Refine README phrasing to avoid over-indexing
description: Update the Tool Calling Synthesis section to sound less promotional ('Unlike naive wrappers...') and more descriptive of the actual mechanical behavior. Also clarify the example policy JSON to show exactly what's currently in fanout_policies.json so users aren't confused by models that aren't actually configured by default.

title: Improve README documentation
description: Expand README to include Configuration/Env vars, Policy Configuration schema/explanation, and Tool Calling Synthesis behavior as agreed with the user.

title: Improve synthesis prompt for tool calls
description: The synthesis model sometimes interprets '[Tool Call: get_weather(...)]' as text to be output literally, rather than actually invoking the tool using the tool calling API. We should improve the build_synthesis_prompt instructions to explicitly instruct the model to use the tool calling mechanism if the candidates agreed on a tool call.

title: Remove fan-out bypass for tool-calling requests
description: Now that candidate tool calls are passed to the synthesis model, remove the early-return logic in orchestrator.py complete_chat and stream_chat that bypasses fan-out when the request contains 'tools'. Ensure the test_parity checks for tool calls continue to pass with synthesis enabled.

title: Serialize candidate tool calls into the synthesis prompt
description: In orchestrator.py, update how candidate responses are formatted for the synthesis prompt. If a candidate returns tool calls, they should be converted to a readable text format (e.g., JSON representation) and included in the <candidate_n> block so the synthesis model can see what tools the candidates decided to call. Also, pass the tools configuration to the synthesis request so the synthesizer can emit the final tool calls.

title: Synthesize tool calls instead of bypassing fan-out
description: Currently, if a request contains 'tools', we bypass fan-out and just hit the fallback model. If candidates return 'tool_calls', we just pass the first one through without synthesis. The user wants true synthesis of both text responses AND tool calls. We need to allow fan-out for requests with tools, serialize candidate tool calls into the synthesis prompt, and provide the tools to the synthesis model so it can emit its own synthesized tool calls.

title: Preserve response_format in synthesis request
description: Currently, models.py strips response_format from the synthesis_request_body. This breaks strict schema adherence for the final synthesized response if the user requests json_schema.

title: Add parity coverage for stream_options: include_usage
description: Ensure that if a client passes stream_options: {include_usage: true}, our facade accurately forwards it and maintains wire-parity with OpenRouter's behavior. We should add a test in test_parity.py to verify.

title: Epic: Request Caching Layer
description: Implement a caching layer (e.g., Redis or local disk/memory) to serve identical requests without hitting upstream models. This will save costs and drastically reduce latency for repeated synthesis tasks.

title: Epic: Observability and Telemetry
description: Implement robust metrics and tracing (e.g., OpenTelemetry, Prometheus endpoint). A proxy needs deep visibility into latency, token usage, candidate success/fail rates, and upstream error tracking.

title: Implement Load Balancing and Rate Limiting
description: Ensure the service scales gracefully by implementing basic per-user rate-limiting and ensuring upstream API requests are load-balanced properly among different configured candidate policies.
close_reason: Implemented in-memory sliding window rate-limiter in app.py with configurable RPM. Updated orchestrator.py distribute_models to use random.sample for true stochastic load-balancing across upstream candidates.

title: Epic: OpenRouter Streaming Parity Refinement
description: Investigate edge cases with server-sent events to ensure 100% adherence to OpenRouter documentation.

value: All parity tests green with Haiku. Ready to proceed to next epic.
